title: Diagram update queue with debouncing and batching
purpose: Manages batched diagram updates with debouncing to reduce API calls and improve performance during rapid modifications.
module_context: Exports a React hook that provides queueing and debouncing functionality for diagram updates with configurable delay and immediate flush capability.

methods.name: useDiagramUpdateQueue
methods.normalized_params: (updateDiagram: (id: string, updates: { content: string; lastModified: number }): void, options: UseDiagramUpdateQueueOptions): { queueUpdate: (id: string, content: string, lastModified: number) => void; flushNow: () => void }

steps.content: Initialize the pending updates map and debounce timer reference.
Store the updateDiagram callback in a ref to prevent effect re-runs.
Return an object containing queueUpdate and flushNow functions.
Check if there are any pending updates to process.
Apply each pending update by calling the updateDiagram callback.
Clear the pending updates map after applying all updates.

methods.name: queueUpdate
methods.normalized_params: (id: string, content: string, lastModified: number): void

steps.content: Add or replace the update in the pending map for the given diagram ID.
Clear any existing debounce timer if present.
Set a new debounce timer to flush pending updates after the configured delay.
Cancel any pending debounce timer if present.
Immediately flush all pending updates by calling the flush function.
Clear any existing debounce timer on component unmount.
